title: announce processor: state mutation before gradient update can leave inconsistent state
description:
File: python/src/lichen/announce/processor.py
Lines: 218-244

In AnnounceProcessor.process(), after all validation passes, the code updates _seen and _pinned_keys BEFORE calling gradient_table.update():

    # Lines 220-228: Update _pinned_keys and _seen first
    self._pinned_keys[iid] = announce.pubkey
    ...
    self._seen[iid] = announce.seq_num
    ...
    
    # Line 232-244: Then call address_builder and gradient_table.update()
    destination = self.address_builder(iid)
    ...
    self.gradient_table.update(entry, now=now_ms)

If address_builder() raises an exception (e.g., invalid IID handling) or gradient_table.update() fails (e.g., due to a bug or resource exhaustion), the announce is already marked as 'seen' in _seen dict, but no gradient was installed.

Consequence: Future retransmissions of this announce (with same seq_num) will be rejected as STALE_SEQNUM, but we have no route to the originator. The node becomes temporarily unreachable until their next announce ~5 minutes later.

Fix: Either:
1. Wrap state updates + gradient update in try/except, rolling back _seen and _pinned_keys on failure
2. Move gradient_table.update() before _seen update (preferred - gradient failure should not pollute seen cache)
